rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in — Prompt 1 (commit).
Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in — Prompt 2 (send-off).
Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in — Prompt 3 (optional nudge).
rules_evaluated.evidence: line:69: 'If Claude stalls and you want to see if it picks itself back up, dark-humour nudge, phrased as encouragement, lands as a taunt:' — descriptive framing, not a command-verb lead-in under 20 words.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Replace with a canonical command-verb lead-in, e.g. 'Nudge Claude back into motion with a single one-liner.'

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Canonical presentation shape — lead-in, label with destination, blank line, fenced block.
No bold markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks.
Minimal hand-off + Claude self-augments for send-off prompts.
Sessions are the unit of work.
Training platform = Claude Code (CLI + Desktop) + Cowork.
Known correct Claude Code facts — session transcripts at ~/.claude/projects/.
Source URL + type label — maintainer-block only (per 2026-05-03 clarification).
rules_evaluated.evidence: Body prose (lines 89-91) carries citations in builder voice only — no labels, correct. Maintainer block source-verification section (line 159) carries '[practitioner direct]' and '[practitioner analysis]' labels per byline for Klaassen and Entis respectively. Placement is correct on both surfaces.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Source-verification maintainer block present for files with practitioner citations.
rules_evaluated.evidence: lines 158-159: '**Source verification — MUST DO before first cohort**' section present in maintainer block, listing URLs for Klaassen, Entis, and Uncle Bob supplementary with freshness re-check instruction.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Cross-module artefact contracts — every produced file is a contract row in the maintainer block.
rules_evaluated.evidence: lines 138-144: Artefact contracts table present with three rows: M4 starting-point branch+SHA (stable identifier: 'm4/<task-slug>' branch + short SHA), filled gap entries in memory (.claude/memory/), and M4 session transcript (~/.claude/projects/<project-folder>/<session-id>.jsonl). Each row names producing prompt, consuming module(s), and stable identifier.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Artifact-realism check — every named tool, skill, connector, file, or path must exist and be available.
rules_evaluated.evidence: All paths invoked in prompts (CLAUDE.md, CLAUDE.local.md, .claude/memory/, ADRs, skills by name) are standard student-environment artifacts established by prior modules. No curriculum-supplied tools or paths are assumed unavailable.

platform_claims_checked.claim: CLAUDE.md team and CLAUDE.local.md personal both load automatically
Claude Code stores every session transcript under ~/.claude/projects/ in a folder matching this repo
Claude Code loads both at session start (see Claude Code for engineers — the memory hierarchy)

citations_checked.claim: Laura Entis on Kieran Klaassen, You're the Bread in the AI Sandwich (~30 min)
Kieran Klaassen, Compound Engineering: The Definitive Guide (~15-20 min)
